methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oklo is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $10B+ after raising $500M+. The nuclear energy sector comprises a sparse peer universe of 3 public companies and 8 private firms, including notable private peers such as TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Zap Energy.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 7 same-sector comparables total, insufficient to establish a robust peer set. When filtered for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies, the filtered peer set falls below the three-datapoint minimum required for meaningful statistical triangulation. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized capital intensity and regulatory environment of nuclear energy development.

The sparsity of the comparable universe—particularly the limited number of public nuclear energy companies (3 tickers: OKLO, NNE, SMR) and the nascent, heterogeneous stage distribution of private nuclear peers—prevents traditional valuation methodology from yielding a defensible range. Any valuation conclusion would rest on deal precedent analysis, discounted cash flow modeling tied to project-specific deployment timelines, or qualitative assessment of relative technology and regulatory positioning. The current $10B+ valuation cannot be validated or challenged using standard peer multiples given data constraints.
